_metadata.title: OKT (Obat Keras Tertentu) Rule Definitions
_metadata.description: Rules for determining if a prescription drug (Obat Keras) can be dispensed by a pharmacist without a doctor's prescription under Indonesian law

regulatory_basis.primary: Pasal 320 ayat (5) UU No. 17 Tahun 2023 tentang Kesehatan
regulatory_basis.technical: Permenkes No. 5 Tahun 2026 tentang Perbekalan Kesehatan (revokes Permenkes 919/1993)
regulatory_basis.note: The formal OKT list (daftar Obat Keras Tertentu) per Pasal 922(3) PP 28/2024 has not been published as of June 2026. This algorithm uses the generic criteria from PP 28/2024 Pasal 922(2) and clinical/pharmacological reasoning.

hard_exclusions.description: Drugs that are ALWAYS excluded from OKT eligibility regardless of other criteria

rules.name: Narcotics Exclusion
rules.rationale: Narcotics (Narkotika Gol. I, II, III) require strict controlled prescription (blanko narkotika) and are explicitly excluded from pharmacist dispensing without prescription

rules.name: Psychotropics Exclusion
rules.rationale: Psychotropics (Psikotropika Gol. I, II, III, IV) are explicitly excluded from pharmacist dispensing without prescription; even electronic prescriptions via telemedicine are prohibited

rules.name: Not Obat Keras
rules.rationale: Drug is already available without prescription; OKT rules do not apply

eligibility_pillars.description: Three eligibility criteria per Pasal 922(2) PP 28/2024. A drug must satisfy AT LEAST ONE pillar to qualify, AND pass all safety gates.

self_medication.name: Swamedikasi (Self-Medication)
self_medication.description: Drugs suitable for self-medication of common, self-recognizable conditions
criteria: Target condition is self-recognizable by patient (e.g., fever, pain, cough, heartburn)
Condition is acute, self-limiting, and does not require diagnostic confirmation
Drug has well-established safety profile with wide therapeutic index
Low risk of masking serious underlying disease
Short-term use (typically ≤7-14 days)
Minimal drug-drug interaction risk with common OTC products
No requirement for therapeutic drug monitoring (TDM)
Standardized dosing (not weight-based or requiring titration)

chronic_refill.name: Resep Ulangan (Chronic Disease Refill)
chronic_refill.description: Drugs for stable chronic conditions where pharmacist can provide refills after initial diagnosis by physician
criteria: Condition is chronic and stable (e.g., hypertension, diabetes, dyslipidemia)
Patient has established diagnosis by physician with prior valid prescription
Dosage is stable and not requiring frequent adjustment
Clinical monitoring can be performed by pharmacist (e.g., blood pressure, blood glucose)
Drug is included in National Formulary (Formularium Nasional) for the indication
Risk of adverse events is manageable without physician intervention
Contraindications can be screened by pharmacist via patient interview

topical.name: Topical / Obat Luar
topical.description: Drugs applied topically for local effect with minimal systemic absorption
criteria: Route of administration is topical (cutaneous, ophthalmic, otic, nasal, rectal, vaginal)
Minimal systemic absorption (bioavailability <5%)
Local action at application site
Low risk of systemic adverse effects
Patient can self-apply with simple instructions
Low abuse potential

safety_gates.description: Safety checks applied regardless of which pillar the drug qualifies under. FAILURE on ANY gate = REJECTED.

gates.name: Narrow Therapeutic Index (NTI)
gates.rationale: NTI drugs require precise dosing and frequent monitoring; pharmacist dispensing without prescription poses unacceptable risk

gates.name: Requires Therapeutic Drug Monitoring (TDM)
gates.rationale: Drugs requiring lab monitoring for safety/efficacy must be managed by physician

gates.name: High Abuse/Dependence Potential (non-scheduled)
gates.rationale: Even if not classified as narcotic/psychotropic, high abuse potential drugs should remain prescription-only

gates.name: Chemotherapy / Immunosuppressant / Biologic
gates.rationale: These require specialist diagnosis, administration, and monitoring

gates.name: Injectable (Parenteral) Route - Except insulin/self-admin
gates.rationale: Parenteral drugs generally require healthcare professional administration

gates.name: Diagnosis-Dependent Therapy
gates.action: REJECT for self-medication; ALLOW for chronic refill if diagnosis established
gates.rationale: Drugs requiring confirmed medical diagnosis cannot be initiated by pharmacist but may be refilled

gates.name: Pregnancy Category D/X or Lactation Risk
gates.action: REJECT for self-medication; CONDITIONAL for chronic refill
gates.rationale: Significant teratogenic risk requires physician counseling

drug_attributes.description: Attribute definitions used by the safety gates

is_nti.description: Drug has narrow therapeutic index (toxic dose close to effective dose)

requires_tdm.description: Drug requires routine therapeutic drug monitoring (lab testing)

self_admin.description: Can the patient self-administer this drug

typical_duration.description: Typical treatment duration for standard indication

default_restrictions.description: Default quantity/duration restrictions applied to OKT dispensing

self_medication_limits.max_quantity: As specified in the drug's OWA history or standard pack size

topical_limits.area_restriction: Must not be applied to large body surface areas if potent corticosteroid

historic_owa.description: Historic Obat Wajib Apotek lists (revoked by Permenkes 3/2021 but useful as reference for self-medication OKT candidates)
historic_owa.note: These lists are no longer legally binding but indicate drugs historically deemed suitable for pharmacist dispensing

strong_okt_candidates.description: Drugs likely eligible under the OKT framework based on PP 28/2024 criteria
self_medication: Salbutamol inhaler (mild intermittent asthma - with pharmacist counseling)
Antihistamines (cetirizine, loratadine for allergy - some already reclassified OTC)
chronic_refill: Insulin (diabetes refill, stable - with blood glucose monitoring)
topical: Betamethasone valerate cream (steroid-responsive dermatoses - limited area/potency)
